name: Heat Stress (Climate-Related)
definition: Heat stress exposure refers to high ambient temperatures and heat events that can contribute to dehydration and physiological strain. In some conditions (e.g., hemoglobinopathies), hydration and heat can be discussed as context for symptom burden at a population level. GENARCH includes this category for education and does not provide medical advice.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Heat can increase physiological stress and dehydration risk; effects depend on access to cooling and hydration, and are not uniform across communities.

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Heat exposure is episodic; vulnerability varies by health status and infrastructure.

references.title: Extreme heat exposure and health outcomes: overview (review)
